result: { "action": "queue", "item": "loop.sh verifier blindness — loop/loop.sh:181 feeds the verifier only `git -C $WT diff` (unstaged, tracked-only) plus a bare `git status --short`, while the worker allowlist at loop/loop.sh:163 grants no `git add`/`git commit`; every task that creates a new file (0034's boundary.py, 0041's test, 0043-audit's report) or edits the vault (a different repo at /root/claude-brain, never in $WT's diff) is structurally unverifiable, which explains 3/3 consecutive verify FAILs with the identical signature and the trust ledger dropping to 5 skills at 0% watch — freezing the shipping path. Fixing the checker pipeline is not in the contract's acts-alone list, so it queues.", "skill": "fix-verifier-diff-visibility", "spec": "Change loop/loop.sh only, ~3 lines, for user review before applying. (1) In the worker step (line ~163), add `Bash(git add:*)` to --allowedTools and instruct workers to `git add -N .` (intent-to-add: registers new files without staging content) before finishing. (2) In the verify step (line ~181), replace the verifier input `$(git -C \"$WT\" diff; git -C \"$WT\" status --short)` with `$(git -C \"$WT\" add -N . && git -C \"$WT\" diff; git -C \"$WT\" status --short)` so untracked file contents appear in the diff the verifier sees. (3) Stop requiring vault-file evidence in worker diffs: conductor specs must phrase vault task-file edits (status: in-progress, Output/notes) as gate-checkable shell commands (e.g. `grep -q 'status: in-progress' /root/claude-brain/tasks/NNNN-*.md`) executed by the host loop, never as items the diff-only verifier must confirm. After the user applies this, the three failed items (0034, 0041, audit-triad-commits) become re-runnable; their watch-tier retries still queue per contract.", "done_when": ["`git -C <wt> add -N . && git -C <wt> diff` on a worktree containing a brand-new file shows that file's full content in the diff output", "loop/loop.sh verify step (line ~181) passes untracked-file contents to the verifier, confirmed by inspecting the diff of loop.sh", "a subsequent tick's verifier verdict on a task that creates a new file cites the new file's contents rather than reporting it as an unverifiable `??` path"] }
